description: Series scoreboard. Each episode runs the same canonical query set (shared/corpus/queries.json) against the same corpus, and reports the same metrics. The improvements are real and reproducible — clone the repo, regenerate the traces, and the numbers should match.

metrics.precision_at_3: Of the top-3 retrieved chunks, how many are in the expected_sources for the query.
metrics.faithfulness: RAGAS faithfulness score on the LLM answer (introduced in EP8, backfilled to earlier episodes).
metrics.answer_relevancy: RAGAS answer_relevancy on the same answer.
metrics.p95_latency_ms: End-to-end latency p95 across the query set.
metrics.cost_per_query_usd: Sum of embedding + retrieval + reranking + LLM cost per query.

ep01.title: What is RAG?
ep01.technique: baseline RAG (recursive 500-char chunks, dense vectors, top-k=3)

ep02.title: Your first RAG pipeline
ep02.technique: same as ep01, end-to-end implementation walkthrough

ep03.title: Reranking
ep03.technique: ep01 baseline + bge-reranker-v2-m3 on top-20 → top-3

ep04.title: Chunking benchmarks
ep04.technique: ep01 baseline with chunker varied: fixed/recursive/semantic/parent-child

ep05.title: Late chunking
ep05.technique: ep04 winner + late chunking on top

ep06.title: Hybrid search
ep06.technique: ep05 + BM25 fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion

ep07.title: Graph RAG with Neo4j
ep07.technique: ep06 + Neo4j graph traversal for multi-hop queries

ep08.title: RAG evaluation
ep08.technique: RAGAS evaluation harness; introduces faithfulness + relevancy metrics

ep09.title: Productionizing RAG
ep09.technique: ep07 + semantic cache + async retrieval + monitoring
